# Water Quality Parameters and Monitoring

> **Type:** reference
> **Domain:** Environmental Health
> **Ref:** https://www.who.int/teams/environment-climate-change-and-health/water-sanitation-and-health
> **Ingested:** 2026-05-25
> **Quality:** curated
> **Tags:** water-quality, WHO, turbidity, pH, monitoring, standards

## Abstract

Water quality monitoring assesses physical, chemical, and biological parameters against national and international standards (WHO Guidelines, EU Water Framework Directive, US Safe Drinking Water Act). Key physical parameters include turbidity (NTU), temperature, conductivity, and TDS. Chemical parameters cover pH (6.5-8.5), DO (>5 mg/L), BOD/COD, nutrients (N, P), heavy metals (Pb, Hg, As, Cd), and emerging contaminants (PFAS, microplastics, pharmaceuticals). Biological monitoring includes coliform bacteria as fecal contamination indicators. IoT-based continuous monitoring with in-situ sensors is increasingly replacing laboratory sampling, though sensor durability and biofouling remain significant challenges.

## Key Concepts

- **Turbidity** — Cloudiness measured by light scattering (NTU), indicator of suspended solids
- **BOD/COD** — Biochemical and Chemical Oxygen Demand, measures organic pollution load
- **Coliforms** — Indicator organisms for fecal contamination (E. coli, total coliforms)
- **Emerging Contaminants** — PFAS, microplastics, pharmaceuticals with no established standards
- **In-Situ Monitoring** — Submersible multi-parameter sondes with optical and electrochemical sensors
